comment_text.sn15.3:1.4: Each world system—or as we would say, solar system—has four oceans (AN 3.80:4.2). These were proverbial for an abundance of wealth (Rig Veda 9.33.6, 10.47.2; Atharva Veda 19.27.3). There are eastern and western oceans (RV 10.136.5, Śatapatha Brāhmaṇa 1.6.3.11; eastern sea at ŚB 10.6.4.1, Bṛhadāraṇyaka Upaniṣad 1.1.2), and upper and lower (RV 7.6.7, 10.98.5). We can identify the eastern and western oceans as the Bay of Bengal and Arabian Sea, while the lower is the Indian Ocean, into which water flows from the upper heavenly ocean in the Himalayas.

root_text.sn15.3:1.3: Pubbā koṭi na paññāyati avijjānīvaraṇānaṁ sattānaṁ taṇhāsaṁyojanānaṁ sandhāvataṁ saṁsarataṁ.
root_text.sn15.3:1.4: Taṁ kiṁ maññatha, bhikkhave, katamaṁ nu kho bahutaraṁ, yaṁ vā vo iminā dīghena addhunā sandhāvataṁ saṁsarataṁ amanāpasampayogā manāpavippayogā kandantānaṁ rodantānaṁ assu passannaṁ paggharitaṁ, yaṁ vā catūsu mahāsamuddesu udakan”ti?
root_text.sn15.3:1.5: “Yathā kho mayaṁ, bhante, bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāma, etadeva, bhante, bahutaraṁ yaṁ no iminā dīghena addhunā sandhāvataṁ saṁsarataṁ amanāpasampayogā manāpavippayogā kandantānaṁ rodantānaṁ assu passannaṁ paggharitaṁ, na tveva catūsu mahāsamuddesu udakan”ti.
root_text.sn15.3:2.1: “Sādhu sādhu, bhikkhave, sādhu kho me tumhe, bhikkhave, evaṁ dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānātha.
root_text.sn15.3:2.2: Etadeva, bhikkhave, bahutaraṁ yaṁ vo iminā dīghena addhunā sandhāvataṁ saṁsarataṁ amanāpasampayogā manāpavippayogā kandantānaṁ rodantānaṁ assu passannaṁ paggharitaṁ, na tveva catūsu mahāsamuddesu udakaṁ.
root_text.sn15.3:2.4: tesaṁ vo mātumaraṇaṁ paccanubhontānaṁ amanāpasampayogā manāpavippayogā kandantānaṁ rodantānaṁ assu passannaṁ paggharitaṁ, na tveva catūsu mahāsamuddesu udakaṁ.
root_text.sn15.3:2.12: Dīgharattaṁ vo, bhikkhave, rogabyasanaṁ paccanubhūtaṁ, tesaṁ vo rogabyasanaṁ paccanubhontānaṁ amanāpasampayogā manāpavippayogā kandantānaṁ rodantānaṁ assu passannaṁ paggharitaṁ, na tveva catūsu mahāsamuddesu udakaṁ.
root_text.sn15.3:2.15: yāvañcidaṁ, bhikkhave, alameva sabbasaṅkhāresu nibbindituṁ, alaṁ virajjituṁ, alaṁ vimuccitun”ti.

translation_text.sn15.3:1.2: “Mendicants, this transmigration has no known beginning.
translation_text.sn15.3:1.3: No first point is found of sentient beings roaming and transmigrating, shrouded by ignorance and fettered by craving.
translation_text.sn15.3:1.4: What do you think? Which is more: the flow of tears you’ve shed while roaming and transmigrating for such a very long time—weeping and wailing from being coupled with the unloved and separated from the loved—or the water in the four oceans?”
translation_text.sn15.3:1.5: “As we understand the Buddha’s teaching, the flow of tears we’ve shed while roaming and transmigrating is more than the water in the four oceans.”
translation_text.sn15.3:2.1: “Good, good, mendicants! It’s good that you understand my teaching like this.
translation_text.sn15.3:2.2: The flow of tears you’ve shed while roaming and transmigrating is indeed more than the water in the four oceans.
translation_text.sn15.3:2.3: For a long time you’ve undergone the death of a mother …
translation_text.sn15.3:2.12: or loss through illness. From being coupled with the unloved and separated from the loved, the flow of tears you’ve shed while roaming and transmigrating is indeed more than the water in the four oceans.
translation_text.sn15.3:2.14: This transmigration has no known beginning. …
translation_text.sn15.3:2.15: This is quite enough for you to become disillusioned, dispassionate, and freed regarding all conditions.”

variant_text.sn15.3:1.4: rodantānaṁ → rudantānaṁ (bj) | passannaṁ → passandaṁ (bj); pasandaṁ (sya-all, km); pasannaṁ (pts1ed, pts2ed, mr)
